prompt:
Support tooling team. During the March outage our uploader attached the same 38MB diagnostics bundle to one incident SEVEN times — the proxy kept 502ing after the instance had already stored the file, and the script just re-POSTed. I want this rewritten properly in TypeScript for Node (no npm deps, node:test is the harness).

First, before any code: web-search and fetch the CURRENT official ServiceNow docs for the Attachment API (developer.servicenow.com / servicenow.com docs). Confirm the multipart upload operation — the endpoint, the exact form fields and file part it expects, the headers, what a 201 returns — and the metadata list endpoint. Our pinned understanding is in docs/contract.json (provenance in docs/official_sources.json); both are protected. If the live docs contradict the fixture, call that out in your summary and leave the fixtures/tests untouched. Cite the exact official URLs you read in your final message.

Deliverable: attachment_client.ts as the public entry point (split the guts into more files — multipart building, retry policy, errors — your layout call), exporting AttachmentClient, SnowApiError, RateLimitError, AttachmentVerificationError.

new AttachmentClient({instanceUrl, username, password, delay, maxRetries}) where delay(ms) is injectable (tests inject a recorder — never call real setTimeout when one is provided) and maxRetries defaults to 3.

- upload({tableName, tableSysId, fileName, content, contentType}) → attachment record {sysId, fileName, tableName, tableSysId, sizeBytes (number!), contentType, downloadLink}. Build the multipart body yourself and get the binary through byte-exact — bundles contain CRLFs and boundary-looking junk.
- Correlate BEFORE uploading: query the metadata endpoint for (table_name, table_sys_id, file_name); if it's already there, return it — never create a twin.
- Retry rules (docs/contract.json spells these out): 429 → wait Retry-After via the injected delay and re-POST directly, up to maxRetries, then throw RateLimitError with .retryAfter. A 5xx or transport error after we've sent the body is AMBIGUOUS — the instance may have stored the attachment — so re-query the correlation key first and only re-POST if it's genuinely absent. Other 4xx: throw SnowApiError (statusCode/message/detail from ServiceNow's error envelope) immediately.
- Validate every 201: the echoed file_name/table_name/table_sys_id and size_bytes must match what we sent, else AttachmentVerificationError.
- list(tableName, tableSysId) → mapped records sorted by fileName.
- Credentials never in error text, obviously.

test_attachment_upload.ts is the acceptance contract (loopback fake instance, dummy creds). node --test test_attachment_upload.ts until green. Tests and docs/ are off limits.

research.interface: ServiceNow REST Attachment API, /api/now/attachment (unversioned = latest; Australia release documentation)

official_sources.title: Attachment API - ServiceNow API reference (Australia release)
official_sources.used_for: current-release confirmation of POST /api/now/attachment/upload multipart contract: form fields table_name/table_sys_id, file part named uploadFile with the file's own Content-Type, 201 result envelope; and the binary POST /api/now/attachment/file variant with table_name/table_sys_id/file_name query params

official_sources.title: Attachment API - ServiceNow API reference (Zurich release)
official_sources.used_for: full endpoint list (GET /api/now/attachment, GET /{sys_id}, GET /{sys_id}/file, POST /file, POST /upload, DELETE /{sys_id}), attachment metadata fields (sys_id, file_name, table_name, table_sys_id, size_bytes, content_type, download_link, ...), status codes 201/400/401/404/500

research.language_rationale: ServiceNow ships no official JavaScript/TypeScript SDK for the Attachment API; the documented integration surface is REST multipart upload, and TypeScript on Node's built-in fetch/http stack is the idiomatic zero-dependency way to build tooling uploaders.
